reviews.author: Josh Dobeck
reviews.text: It's cool, if you've never seen anything like it. It's worth your little adventure.. there's not much else in the area other than this huge amount of sand right off the side of the road. There are some vendors there who will rent you sheets of plastic if you want to slide down it, but it's not really anything to build a day around... We had a scooter and we found the 25-minute scooter ride. There was smooth. There's virtually no traffic on that road and it was nice just to get away from the little hustle bustle of the town. On our way back we did find a beautiful Temple and having the freedom to be on a scooter and not on a tour. It was nice to just bop in and do everything on our own time frame

reviews.author: Hannani On
reviews.text: Took a scooter and parked across the road. Paid 10k vnd for the parking instead of 20k vnd (after bargaining of course!). After that, you need to hike up to see the beautiful scenery. It's an easy hike, no worries. If you love watching sunset or sunrise, better go early to avoid crowd. Be prepare to wear slippers or sandals. And bring water too!

reviews.author: Catherine
reviews.text: Some pictures I saw in review seem to be from a different sand dunes, not here. This place is much smaller and there is nothing else but a bit sand, trash and locals selling various Vietnamese snacks. We came on Friday around 12, there were hardly any people. Across the dunes there is a small parking lot and we saw a sign day that for bikes it costs money to park but no price, duh. But when we came and left there was no one to pay to anyway. While going up the hill I saw some trash and when we reached the peak and looked on the other side, it was pretty dirty 🥲 The place itself is nice, it’s just sand but still nice. You can see a bit of sea and nice views. The place isn’t large so it takes max 30 minutes to walk around and take pictures. Overall I wouldn’t say it something mesmerizing but just cool to visit if you are passing by.

reviews.author: Ho Ngoc Tin
reviews.text: Very nice place for kid to play, specially at sunset time, do not rent sliding sheet at the gate, just walk to the hills, many there with same price and have people to help you.

reviews.author: V -
reviews.text: I‘m always surprised by other reviews. What do people expect from a Sand Dunes? A Disneyland? Of course it’s endless sand and not a tourist hotspot and if you come with this mindset you won’t be „disappointed“. The sunset is beautiful. Nothing more or less.

reviews.author: Anastasia Sh.
reviews.text: It’s a very beautiful natural place, though it might not feel as special when it’s crowded. We came at sunset and it was perfect. You can sit quietly on the dunes and watch the sea — it’s pure bliss

reviews.author: Thomas Bratty
reviews.text: Good stop to kill 15 minutes. Wouldn’t spend more than that. 50k dong/ board to dune surf. Tip the lady pushing you down the hill.

reviews.author: A. H.
reviews.text: My second visit here. The locals who try to sell you something here or force you to park your motorbike at their place to visit this desert are still quite annoying... otherwise there is a lot of trash in the desert itself, the view of the sea or the sunset is beautiful.

reviews.author: Remko
reviews.text: Beautiful place for sunset. Can be crowded on Beautiful days. Nearby Mui Ne

reviews.author: Walter Ehrmantraut
reviews.text: Happy Lunar New Year! I came here to see the sunrise. The desert isn't big, but it's high enough that you can see all the way to the sea.

reviews.author: Josh Lawrence
reviews.text: It’s a cool place to stop by at if you’re driving down the road. Absolutely not a destination spot, nor a tour area. If you come here with a tour or with the mindset of a must see destination you will be disappointed. With that said, it’s a very cool spot!

reviews.author: Peter
reviews.text: The Red Sand Dunes present a landscape similar to their white counterparts, with beautiful rolling hills of iron-rich, rust-colored sand. However, they offer a more accessible and low-key experience, as there are no formal ATV or jeep tour services here. The main activity is the classic, simple joy of renting a plastic mat and sliding down the warm slopes, making it a perfect spot for a quick, fun stop to enjoy the unique terrain and capture some striking photos, especially at sunset.

reviews.author: Sandra Pfeiffer
reviews.text: Not worth the journey. Sure, you can make it look nice on photos but it's small, you're done in 15 - 20 minutes and there's plastic scattered everywhere. I wish I had just travelled on.

reviews.author: Paul Ventrice
reviews.text: Average sand dune. I think there's better places to go visit that aren't on the map. It's a very interesting land formation though and very windy up there also. I just went there on my own on scooter but maybe the tour will be more interesting. Sad part is the amount of rubbish that's left there and really needs to be cleaned up

reviews.author: Akang
reviews.text: Walking uphill the red sand dunes is a feast, watching sunset at the top is the unforgettable dessert.

reviews.author: Megan Judware
reviews.text: It is a fun experience to ride the atvs on the sand dunes. The guides take great photos of you throughout and help you stear only as needed.

reviews.author: Akarawin Tweekittikul
reviews.text: Another desert in Mui Ne. It is smaller than the White Sand Dunes, and you can try local desserts there.

reviews.author: Andrei Franco
reviews.text: The white sand dunes are definitely cool to see, and the scenery is quite unique. However, the visit is fairly quick unless you opt for activities like riding an ATV or taking a jeep tour—these are fun but can get pricey. Besides these activities, there isn’t much else to do in the area. It’s a great experience to try at least once for the views and adventure, but it’s not something I’d feel the need to revisit. Worth a visit if you’re in the area, though!

reviews.author: Andrew Marvin
reviews.text: Clean thanks to the Pandanus cleaning crew. Locals will follow and harass you to pay $5 to use a trash can lid to slide down the sand dune. Beautiful spot once this problem is addressed.

reviews.author: Egyptian Anubis
reviews.text: If you have desert in your country don’t go You will pay extra money when you get there Beach Buggy like 800000 k which is so much for that

reviews.author: Eric Rumbo
reviews.text: you can do it by yourself but it’s cheap enough to take a tour. really good

reviews.author: Nilou Kedemos
reviews.text: Very pretty! If you’re not doing the jeep tour, go during the day and it’s basically empty! We took some awesome photos.

reviews.author: suraj singh
reviews.text: The Red Sand Dunes in Mũi Né offer a more laid-back, quieter experience compared to the more popular White Sand Dunes. While they don’t have the same vast expanse or major attractions, they still provide a peaceful escape and a chance to enjoy the unique desert landscape. The reddish hue of the sand is striking and makes for great photo opportunities, especially around sunrise and sunset when the light casts beautiful shadows across the dunes. There are some activities available, like sandboarding, though they’re less commercialized and not as intense as at the White Sand Dunes. If you're looking for a more relaxed, short stop to explore the dunes or capture a few photos, it’s a nice place to visit. That said, there's not much else to do here beyond walking around and taking in the view, so it’s best for those who want a quiet break rather than a full-day adventure. If you're in the area and have some spare time, the Red Sand Dunes are worth a brief visit, but don’t expect the same level of excitement or scale as their White counterparts.

reviews.author: James Ng
reviews.text: Family had a blast! Best to go early morning when its not hot and less crowded. Free to wonder around, 50k for sled, and great scenery for desert and ocean view

reviews.author: Adam
reviews.text: Very pretty but wait till you’re at the top to hire a sand board and make sure you have enough to tip the woman or she won’t like you.

reviews.author: Salman Alfarisi
reviews.text: The Red Sand Dunes in Mui Ne are such a vibrant and beautiful spot! Personally, I enjoyed this place more than the White Sand Dunes. It has a unique charm, even though it's smaller and doesn't offer ATV rides like the White Sand Dunes. I visited during the rainy season, and I’d recommend coming in the morning for the best views – the lighting is amazing for photography. The price for sand sliding here is the same as at the White Sand Dunes, costing around 30,000 VND, which is pretty reasonable for the fun experience. Plus, during weekdays, it’s not too crowded, so you can enjoy the scenery without too many people around. I visited the Red Sand Dunes as part of a tour package that also included the White Sand Dunes, Fairy Stream, and the Fishing Village, and I’m glad it was included. It’s definitely a cool place for capturing some great photos, and the overall vibe is really peaceful and relaxing.

reviews.author: Della Rusita
reviews.text: Before I came here, I was in Sand Dune in Doha, and this place has more red kind of color, better sand quality, and it has fantastic weather, too! Just avoid women in the street before the parking lot; she is pushing too much, almost in a situation like a cranky kid who wants us to buy stuff with her. In the end, we only pay parking 20k. But oh God, even my kid doesn't ever push me to do things like she does.

reviews.author: Laurent Renard
reviews.text: this is a small place but if you come early you will enjoy a beautiful light on the sand. Some people rent a ATV but we prefer walk that’s much more fun. After that please visit the beach around and meet the fisherman.

reviews.author: Amira Yusof
reviews.text: There are not only here to play the sand dune. this is just one of it. My driver bring here when I say I want to go to sand dune but so at first we just want to take photo and leave to the ATV place but after we takephotos. there was a local girl offering us to play the sandboarding and my friend want to play it since the price they offer is 50k/board so we think wejust want to rent for 2 board only and just take turns to play. we need to walk few minutes to arrive at place where not so many people and play there. thegirl offer to take our pictures and record videos of us playing that sandboarding. After the session end, she ask for tips and we agree to give tip100k. my friend already give 100k to her but she ask for another 100k to give to her mum. we pissed off because its too much as we are not that rich to givetip 200k easily! but because most of us are soft hearted so we give 50k more to her. that’s all. So moral of the story, if they offer to take your picture,just say no if don’t want to pay tips at the end of the game. if you want to play ATV, you need to go to other place, not here. I will share it at the next review.

reviews.author: Wade Wilson
reviews.text: Red Sand Dunes. Whoever named this place clearly lost a fight with a Crayola box. Spoiler: it’s not that red. It’s more like “slightly dirty orange” or “did someone spill paprika on the beige dunes.” If you came here expecting Mars, congrats, you just landed in your neighbor’s cat litter tray after taco night. What’s actually here? Sand. Shocking, I know. A few locals selling drinks and snacks because, let’s be honest, they know you’ll cave once the sun starts deep-frying your soul. That’s it. No ATVs, no cinematic vistas, just a sandy hill and your own bad life choices. But here’s the plot twist: it’s free. And that alone makes it superior to White Sand Dunes, where you pay actual dongs just to get sunburned and robbed by gravity. Here, you just stroll in, sink into sand that looks like it gave up trying to be red, and pretend you’re cultured while sweating through your clothes. So yeah, Red Sand Dunes: still boring, still sand, but at least your wallet survives. Call it the Costco sample version of tourist traps—cheap, slightly disappointing, but somehow better than the overpriced full product.
